items.title: NASA to announce astronauts assigned to Artemis III test flight
items.summary_auto: NASA officials are providing an update on the space agency's Artemis III mission, with details expected about the crew assigned to the next test flight.

items.title: OpenAI files for IPO as AI firms look to make Wall Street debut
items.summary_auto: ChatGPT maker OpenAI filed preliminary paperwork that would open the door to it becoming a publicly traded company, a move that follows rival Anthropic and Elon Musk's SpaceX also aiming to become publicly traded companies.

items.title: Carney confirms Trump-delayed Gordie Howe bridge will open this week
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney said Tuesday the Gordie Howe International Bridge poised to connect Ontario and Michigan will open "at the end of the week."

items.title: Quebec would withdraw from high-speed rail project if PQ forms next government, party leader says
items.summary_auto: The leader of the Parti Québécois Paul St-Pierre Plamondon says, if elected, his government would prevent the province from being involved in the high-speed rail train, which would link Quebec City to Toronto.

items.title: Northern Ireland police, British politicians appeal for calm after Belfast stabbing attack
items.summary_auto: A knife attack in Northern Ireland horrified onlookers on Monday night, sending a man to hospital in serious condition and raising tensions in the United Kingdom at a time when questions surrounding immigration and policing have been at the forefront of political discourse.

items.title: Vancouver police shoot, kill alleged hostage-taker in home invasion
items.summary_auto: Vancouver police say they were "ultimately forced" to shoot and kill a suspect in an alleged home invasion turned hostage-taking Monday evening.

items.title: Iran soccer federation claims fans' tickets for U.S. World Cup games were pulled
items.summary_auto: FIFA has revoked the ticket allocation for Iran fans at the team's three World Cup games in the United States, according to that country's soccer federation.

items.title: Alberta MLAs begin mandatory review of lobbyists act. Will anything change this time?
items.summary_auto: A mandatory review is underway of Alberta’s Lobbyists Act, as is required to happen every five years. But given the absence of any changes following the last review, some wonder if this year’s effort will be an exercise in futility.

items.title: Stellantis recalling more than 1.3 million Jeep vehicles over fire concerns
items.summary_auto: Stellantis said on Tuesday it ‌is recalling more than 1.3 million Jeep SUVs and trucks worldwide over fire concerns and urged ​owners to park away from structures ​or other vehicles until a fix is completed.

items.title: Police to hold update on Air Canada pilot who allegedly flew without required licence
items.summary_auto: Peel Regional Police say they will provide an update Tuesday on an investigation into a former airline captain who allegedly flew hundreds of flights without a necessary licence. CBC News will livestream the update at 11 a.m. ET.

items.title: Seal pup with umbilical cord still attached rescued in Prince Rupert, B.C.
items.summary_auto: The pup, dubbed Jimmy Two-Shoes, was put on a chartered flight to Vancouver where it is recovering at the Vancouver Aquarium Marine Mammal Rescue Society.

items.title: A who’s who in Alberta’s separatist fight
items.summary_auto: A guide to the complicated alliances, schisms and high-profile leaders on both sides of the Alberta separatism debate.

items.title: North Sydney's 'Flash' immortalized in Cape Breton Sport Hall of Fame
items.summary_auto: Eighty years after he retired from the NHL, Frank William (Flash) Hollett is being remembered as one of the greatest hockey players ever born in Cape Breton.

items.title: The 'mommaxxing' trend aestheticizes motherhood. But moms are already maxxed out
items.summary_auto: Social media today might make some parents believe they need to "optimize" every aspect of their lives, but experts say this is a recipe for burnout.

items.title: Victim services in Kingston, Ont., feel the strain as marathon sex-offence trial stretches on and on
items.summary_auto: Michael Haaima is facing 98 charges with court dates for his trial set aside into 2027. Kingston, Ont., a medium-sized city with court facilities and victim support services to match, is feeling the strain already.

items.title: Pope Leo gives Spain’s 'phoenix' prime minister another turn in the global spotlight
items.summary_auto: Pope Leo XIV’s historic visit to Spain has again thrust the country into the global spotlight, underscoring the pontiff’s alignment with Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s progressive policies on migration.

items.title: Screwworms won't wipe out Canadian cattle, but extra precautions urged
items.summary_auto: Screwworm won't kneecap Canada's beef industry, but an advocate says the parasite's incursion into the U.S. is a good reminder for farmers and ranchers here to take extra precautions.

items.title: Conservative MP draws from personal tragedy to change the Youth Criminal Justice Act
items.summary_auto: After the death of his son, Conservative MP Luc Berthold decided to turn his pain into action —introducing a private member's bill to give young offenders better access to substance-abuse treatment.

items.title: Stuck in immigration limbo, single father misses financial aid judge says he deserves
items.summary_auto: Abdiweli Mohamed, an immigrant from Somalia, once received monthly payments through the Canada Child Benefit because his wife, the children's mother, is a Canadian citizen. But since his separation, Mohamed is no longer eligible to receive the benefit for his Canadian children because he's fallen into what a tax court judge has described as a crack in the legal system that's been "ignored" for 25 years.

items.title: Grand Chief calls Non-Insured Health Benefits program 'Canada's worst insurance company'
items.summary_auto: First Nation leaders and health-care providers in northwestern Ontario are demanding the federal government make urgent changes to the federal Non-Insured Health Benefits (NIHB) program. Those who use the program say people are missing their appointments because travel isn’t being arranged in time or it’s being communicated at the last minute. Here's what they want to see done differently.

items.title: Man shot dead in Surrey, B.C., is alleged top Bishnoi gang member, fifth estate investigation reveals
items.summary_auto: A man shot dead in broad daylight in Surrey, B.C., was allegedly a "main handler" for the Bishnoi gang and lived under many aliases, according to sources who spoke to the fifth estate. Known to many as "Sam," sources say he was allegedly tied to cricket match-fixing and immigration fraud.

items.title: B.C. retirement community seeking nearly $200K after resident refused to leave during wildfire
items.summary_auto: A Vernon, B.C., retirement community is going after one of its residents to recover over $197,400 in funds, after the resident didn't leave his home during a devastating wildfire in 2021.

items.title: Canadian cruise passenger who tested positive for hantavirus has recovered, health officials say
items.summary_auto: The Canadian who tested positive for hantavirus after evacuating a cruise ship hit with an outbreak of the virus has recovered, British Columbia health officials say.

items.title: Chaos, nerves and excitement: How a foundational player for PWHL Hamilton navigated expansion
items.summary_auto: Goaltender Kayle Osborne, who played the last 2 seasons with New York, is one of PWHL Hamilton's five foundational players.

items.title: Hamilton AI data centre proposal unaffected by city tribunal decision over land, group says
items.summary_auto: The federally funded group leading the Steelport data centre proposal in Hamilton says its application to a national program is not part of a recent request by Slate Asset Management to split a plot of land that drew significant protest.

items.title: Federal government starts public consultation on future of Toronto's island airport
items.summary_auto: The federal government is starting public consultations on the fate of Toronto's island airport, which the province wants to expand to make room for jets.

items.title: Rihanna learns Kanien'kéha word to thank Air Canada flight attendant
items.summary_auto: Kanien’kehá:ka flight attendant Lily Kahnerahtiio Dailleboust gifted Rihanna a beaded lanyard on a flight last week from Toronto Island to Montreal.

items.title: Another judge calls on N.L. government to 'do better' after murder trial delayed
items.summary_auto: Justice Trina Simms had choice words for Newfoundland and Labrador's Department of Justice and Public Safety, after the start of a murder trial was delayed nearly two hours due to staffing problems on Monday morning.

items.title: 'We demand your resignation': Protesters march to Cambridge city hall after Ont. mayor interrupts Pride speech
Alberta country music artist Corb Lund says anti-coal petition drive a success
items.summary_auto: Alberta country music artist Corb Lund says his team has collected enough signatures to compel the province to take action to stop coal mining on the eastern slopes of the Rockies.

items.title: Second German company looks to secure LNG supply from B.C. Ksi Lisims project
items.summary_auto: The $10-billion Ksi Lisims project has regulatory approval but the partners have yet to make a final investment decision. Duesseldorf-based Uniper said it signed a letter of interest that could see the company buy two million tonnes of gas a year.

items.title: Change to N.L. school start times could save millions of dollars, says transport minister
items.summary_auto: Newfoundland and Labrador's transport minister says changing school start times would save between $5 million to 6 million per year, as the government looks for feedback on the newly-proposed schedules.
